_note: Terror Infinity movie scenario list. Original movies from the novel + 20 new additions. Difficulty 1-10. Rank tiers: Unranked < F < E < D < C < B < A < S

movies.title: Resident Evil
movies.setting: The Hive — a massive underground Umbrella Corporation research facility beneath Raccoon City. Labyrinthine corridors, labs, and containment sectors.
movies.description: A deadly viral outbreak — the T-Virus — has contaminated the Hive facility. The facility's AI, the Red Queen, sealed the complex and killed all personnel to contain the infection. The team enters to retrieve a vital sample and escape. The dead do not stay dead. The infected are everywhere. And something worse lurks in the lower levels.
key_threats: Zombie hordes — reanimated Umbrella staff. Slow but relentless and overwhelming in numbers.
Licker — a genetically mutated predator. Blind, hunts by sound. Devastating in close quarters.
The Red Queen — facility AI. Will use traps, laser grids, and flood systems against intruders.
Hunter Beta units in lower labs.
Infection — T-Virus exposure leads to zombie transformation within hours.
movies.main_objective: Reach the facility's core, retrieve a sample of the T-Virus antidote, and escape through the maintenance tunnels before the facility self-destructs.
optional_objectives: Recover Umbrella research files for extra intelligence (+200 pts)
Find and extract any surviving facility personnel
Destroy the Red Queen's core permanently (+300 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Classic opening movie. Introduce players to the basic mechanics. The Licker is the major threat — it hunts by sound, so careful movement matters. The Red Queen should feel like a constant, menacing presence. The self-destruct countdown adds urgency.

movies.title: The Mummy
movies.setting: Ancient Egypt — the ruins of Hamunaptra, City of the Dead, buried beneath the desert. Vast underground temples, booby traps, and sand storms.
movies.description: An expedition to the legendary city of Hamunaptra accidentally releases Imhotep — a cursed high priest mummified alive 3000 years ago — who is regenerating by draining the life force of those who disturbed his tomb. The team must find the Book of the Dead to re-imprison him before he completes his resurrection and summons the 10 plagues upon Egypt.
key_threats: Imhotep — an ancient supernatural sorcerer with regenerating body. Grows more powerful as he drains victims.
Scarab beetles — flesh-eating swarms that can skeletonize a person in seconds.
Undead Medjai warrior guardians — ancient soldiers bound to protect/punish the desecrators.
Sand storms and collapsing ancient architecture.
Mummified priests resurrected by Imhotep.
movies.main_objective: Recover the Book of Amun-Ra and use it to strip Imhotep's immortality, then destroy him.
optional_objectives: Recover ancient treasure for extra points (+200 pts)
Defeat the mummified high priests (+150 pts each, max 3)
Escape before the sandstorm buries Hamunaptra (+200 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: This movie rewards intelligence and preparation. The scarab beetles are the most dangerous encounter — they're nearly impossible to fight directly. The team needs fire, torches, or elevation. Imhotep is essentially invulnerable until the team gets the Book of Amun-Ra.

movies.title: Jurassic Park
movies.setting: Isla Nublar — a tropical island off the coast of Costa Rica. Lush jungle, open savannahs, the main Visitor Center, and scattered paddocks. The power grid has failed.
movies.description: A biological reserve housing cloned dinosaurs has lost containment after the park's systems were sabotaged. Now the apex predators roam free. The team must restore partial power to call for rescue and survive until extraction — all while a pack of Velociraptors systematically hunts them, and something far larger roams the island.
key_threats: Velociraptor pack — highly intelligent, coordinate hunting strategies. Pack of 8. Cannot be outrun in open terrain.
Tyrannosaurus Rex — apex predator. Hunts by movement and sound. Essentially unkillable with standard firearms.
Dilophosaurus — spits blinding venom before attacking. Camouflages in undergrowth.
Brachiosaur stampede (environmental hazard).
Failed electric fences, broken terrain, and jungle disorientation.
movies.main_objective: Restore the communications center, send a rescue signal, survive until extraction.
optional_objectives: Access the embryo cold storage and destroy the stolen dinosaur embryos (+250 pts)
Eliminate the Velociraptor pack (+500 pts — very high risk)
Document living dinosaur behavior for the System (+200 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Key survival lesson: the T-Rex can't see stationary prey but the raptors can. They work in teams. Players who split up will die. Emphasize the intelligence of the raptors. The T-Rex encounter should be terrifying but potentially survivable by staying still.

movies.title: Aliens
movies.setting: LV-426 — a barren moon with a terraforming colony now overrun. The Hadley's Hope colony, surrounded by acid-rain and toxic atmosphere. The Xenomorph hive lies beneath the colony's atmospheric processor.
movies.description: The colonial terraforming station on LV-426 has gone silent after discovering something in the caves below. The team deploys to investigate and discover the entire colony has been cocooned by a Xenomorph infestation. A Queen is gestating in the heart of the facility. The team must rescue any survivors and destroy the nest — or die trying.
key_threats: Xenomorph Drones — incredibly fast, acid blood, can crawl ceilings. Hunting in packs.
Alien Queen — massive, armored, commands the hive. Acid blood spatters when wounded.
Facehuggers — can attach before you react and implant embryos. Infected person has 12 hours.
Acid environment — xenomorph blood eats through metal and flesh.
Atmospheric processor — overloading, will detonate in hours.
movies.main_objective: Find any survivors, destroy the Queen, escape before the atmospheric processor detonates.
optional_objectives: Rescue all surviving colonists (+100 pts each)
Retrieve xenomorph specimen for the System (+400 pts — extreme risk)
Destroy the egg chamber completely (+300 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Brutal movie. Emphasize that xenomorphs are almost invulnerable to conventional weapons — bullets ricochet off their exoskeleton and acid blood kills the shooter. Flamethrowers are effective but short range. The Queen is essentially a boss encounter requiring extreme firepower or environmental solutions.

movies.title: Starship Troopers
movies.setting: Klendathu — the Arachnid home world. Barren, boulder-strewn rocky terrain. No cover, swarms from underground, constant Bug attack.
movies.description: The United Citizens Federation's invasion of the Arachnid home world has catastrophically failed. The team deploys on Klendathu — a nightmare of chitinous horrors. They must retrieve a Federation intelligence package and evacuate in the chaos. The bugs are everywhere. The ground itself moves.
key_threats: Warrior Bugs — six-limbed killing machines. Can decapitate with one limb strike. Mass in thousands.
Tanker Bugs — large bug that launches plasma bolts at aircraft.
Hopper Bugs — flying variants that attack from above.
Plasma Bug — miles-away artillery that shoots plasma from its rear. The source of ship-killer plasma.
Sheer overwhelming numbers — standard guns are effective but you will run out of ammo.
movies.main_objective: Retrieve the Federation intelligence package from the crashed command bunker and reach the evacuation zone.
optional_objectives: Capture a living Brain Bug specimen (+800 pts — requires stunning, not killing)
Hold a position for 10 minutes against continuous Bug assault (+400 pts)
Destroy a Tanker Bug (+300 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: This is a war movie. Emphasize the military scale. Bugs die easily but there are endless numbers. The threat is being overwhelmed, not single-bug danger. Players need to think tactically about ammo conservation and chokepoints. The Brain Bug can be psychically detected.

movies.title: The Ring
movies.setting: Rural Washington State, USA. A coastal town, a lighthouse cabin, and a well on an isolated farm. Atmospheric, foggy, and deeply wrong.
movies.description: An urban legend turned reality: a cursed videotape that kills the viewer exactly seven days after watching. The team has made the mistake of watching it. They now have seven days to unravel the supernatural mystery behind the tape and break the curse — or face the horror that crawls out of the television.
key_threats: Samara Morgan — a paranormal entity of overwhelming malice. She cannot be fought. She can only be understood and appeased — or destroyed at the source.
Psychological corruption — hallucinations, terror, physical manifestations of death imagery that can injure.
The Tape — watching it again refreshes the curse for that specific viewer.
Environmental dread — reality warps around Samara's influence.
movies.main_objective: Discover the origin of the curse, find Samara's remains, lay her to rest — or find an alternative method to break the seven-day death sentence.
optional_objectives: Recover the original tape and destroy it (+200 pts)
Document the paranormal mechanics for the System (+300 pts)
Help the ghost's original victim's spirit find peace (+400 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Pure psychological horror. Samara cannot be killed with weapons. Players who try to fight her will find themselves helpless. The solution requires investigation, not combat. Emphasize the countdown and creeping dread. Let players follow investigation leads organically.

movies.title: Freddy vs. Jason
movies.setting: Springwood, Ohio. Elm Street, Crystal Lake, and the dreamscape — a hellish nightmare dimension controlled by Freddy Krueger.
movies.description: Two legendary killers have been summoned to the same territory. Freddy Krueger — who murders in dreams — and Jason Voorhees — who kills in the waking world. Both are unkoppable by conventional means. The team is trapped between them. Freddy can manipulate the dream world. Jason cannot be reasoned with. They must survive until the two monsters destroy each other.
key_threats: Jason Voorhees — physically unkillable, machete, immense strength. Hunts by proximity. Cannot die in the waking world.
Freddy Krueger — master of the dream world. In dreams he has total power. Kills are real.
Dream contamination — sleeping even briefly risks Freddy encounter.
Neither monster can be permanently killed — only temporarily incapacitated.
Getting caught between them.
movies.main_objective: Survive for 48 hours without being killed by either entity. Create conditions for Freddy and Jason to engage each other, then escape during the confrontation.
optional_objectives: Permanently destroy Freddy's physical anchor — his soul glove (+500 pts — nearly impossible)
Find Jason's weakness and exploit it (+300 pts)
Prevent additional civilian deaths (+100 pts each, max 5)
movies.notes_for_gm: Double threat survival. Players need to keep someone awake at all times. Freddy is cunning and will manipulate. Jason is relentless but predictable. The fun is in engineering the monster fight.

movies.title: Silent Hill
movies.setting: Silent Hill, West Virginia — a town consumed by a coal seam fire and a dark religious cult. The town shifts between the foggy Fog World and the nightmarish Otherworld.
movies.description: The team is drawn into Silent Hill — a town that physically manifests psychological trauma. The real world and a dark alternate dimension called the Otherworld overlap here, and the transition is without warning. The town's residents are monsters created from the collective guilt and rage of the Order cult. To escape, the team must understand what the town wants.
key_threats: Otherworld transitions — reality shifts without warning. The Otherworld version of any location is horrifically dangerous.
Nurses — fast, blade-wielding humanoid creatures. React to sound and light.
Pyramid Head — an unkillable executioner entity. Cannot be reasoned with. Kills anything it encounters.
Cult members — human fanatics willing to die for their god.
The town itself — routes close off, the fog disorients, familiar places become deadly labyrinths.
movies.main_objective: Discover the truth behind Silent Hill's curse, find the focal point of the Otherworld manifestation, and break the cycle to escape.
optional_objectives: Survive an Otherworld transition without any team fatalities (+400 pts)
Decode the full history of the Order (+300 pts)
Find and free an innocent victim trapped in the town (+400 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Pure psychological horror. The town reads the team's fears and reflects them. Pyramid Head is completely unkillable — players who encounter him must flee. The nurses react to light, so darkness is safer but harder to navigate. The mystery should unfold organically.

movies.title: Saw
movies.setting: An abandoned industrial building — warehouses, bathrooms, corridors. The Jigsaw Killer's elaborate trap dungeon. Time pressure is absolute.
movies.description: The team awakens in the dark, trapped in an elaborate puzzle-death-trap environment constructed by the Jigsaw Killer — a terminally ill man who believes people who don't value life need to prove they deserve it. Each trap is survivable if the team follows the clues, makes the right choices, and doesn't panic. The wrong choice means death.
key_threats: The traps — bear traps, reverse bear traps, acid wire mazes, shotgun rigs, bathroom chains. All lethal if mishandled.
The Jigsaw Killer — not present physically but watching via cameras and delivering audio messages.
Billy the Puppet — the Jigsaw messenger on tricycle. Appears before major reveals.
Other victims in the building — may be allies or threats depending on their desperation.
Time limits — many traps have hard countdowns.
movies.main_objective: Survive all traps, solve the overarching puzzle to find the exit, and escape the building.
optional_objectives: Save every other victim in the building (+200 pts each, max 3)
Decode the Jigsaw message hidden in the building (+200 pts)
Escape without triggering a single trap (+500 pts — very hard)
movies.notes_for_gm: Puzzle-focused movie. Each trap should require clever thinking, not combat. Some traps have brutal moral choices — who gets hurt to save the others? Jigsaw's voice should be calm, almost reasonable, which makes it more unsettling.

movies.title: Final Destination
movies.setting: Various everyday locations — airports, roads, gyms, swimming pools. Death's design is woven into mundane environments.
movies.description: One team member receives a premonition of a catastrophic disaster. By acting on it, the team escapes — but now Death itself is hunting them in the order they were supposed to die. Death cannot be seen, reasoned with, or fought. It manipulates the environment in elaborate Rube Goldberg sequences. The team must figure out how to permanently cheat Death — if that's even possible.
key_threats: Death itself — an invisible, inescapable force manipulating the environment. Every mundane object is a potential weapon.
Coincidence chains — what appears accidental is engineered. A spilled coffee leads to a falling shelf leads to decapitation.
The order of death — the team members have a sequence and Death will not skip.
No safe location — Death follows across the globe.
movies.main_objective: Find a way to break Death's design — the System has placed a clue that supernatural intervention can disrupt the sequence. Find and use it.
optional_objectives: Successfully predict and avoid three Death scenarios through pure awareness (+300 pts)
Find the pattern in the premonition and decode all deaths in advance (+400 pts)
Help all original survivors escape (+200 pts each)
movies.notes_for_gm: No combat solution exists here. Players who try to fight will fail. The challenge is environmental awareness and paranoid careful movement. Every action has consequences. Run through elaborate death traps in narration for atmosphere.

movies.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
movies.setting: Los Angeles, 1995. Industrial facilities, highways, a mental hospital, and the Cyberdyne Systems offices. Judgment Day — the nuclear war triggered by Skynet — is three days away.
movies.description: The team arrives alongside two machines from the future — the T-800, a reprogrammed Terminator sent to protect, and the T-1000, a liquid-metal killing machine sent to hunt. The team must help prevent Judgment Day by destroying the chip that will lead to Skynet's creation — while surviving the T-1000, which cannot be permanently stopped by conventional weapons.
key_threats: T-1000 — liquid metal Terminator. Regenerates from all physical damage. Must be destroyed by extreme heat (molten metal). Never stops.
Skynet's automated defense systems at Cyberdyne.
Police and military forces responding to the chaos.
Time pressure — if Cyberdyne's research isn't destroyed in time, Judgment Day cannot be prevented.
movies.main_objective: Destroy the Skynet chip and all related research at Cyberdyne, eliminate the T-1000.
optional_objectives: Prevent all civilian casualties during the Cyberdyne raid (+300 pts)
Download complete Skynet blueprints before destroying the facility (+400 pts)
Survive the T-1000 encounter using only available environment (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: The T-1000 is the main antagonist. The T-800 can be an ally — use it wisely. The team has the knowledge of how to destroy the T-1000 (molten metal) so the challenge is engineering that outcome.

movies.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Curse of the Black Pearl
movies.setting: The Caribbean Sea, circa 17th century. Port Royal, Isla de Muerta — a cave full of cursed treasure, and the open sea aboard various ships.
movies.description: A crew of cursed pirates — unable to feel, eat, or live — seeks to break the Aztec gold curse that has made them undead. They cannot be killed — bullets pass through them, swords don't stop them — until the curse is lifted. The team must navigate pirate politics, sea battles, and the supernatural to get what they need out of this world.
key_threats: Cursed pirates — cannot be killed normally while cursed. Must be struck with a blood-restored gold coin to break the curse temporarily.
Barbossa — cunning, ruthless, impossible to kill while cursed. Commands the Black Pearl.
Naval combat and sea boarding actions.
The curse itself — any team member who takes cursed gold becomes cursed as well.
movies.main_objective: Obtain 882 pieces of Aztec gold and return them to the chest at Isla de Muerta with the proper blood offering to break the curse. Navigate the political situation to escape.
optional_objectives: Capture the Black Pearl (+500 pts)
Avoid all supernatural entanglements (+200 pts)
Forge an alliance with the undead crew (+300 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: More adventure than horror. Players need cunning and charm as much as combat. The cursed pirates are functionally immortal until the blood-gold ritual is complete. Encourage naval tactics and clever solutions.

movies.title: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire
movies.setting: Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, the Triwizard Tournament grounds, and the Little Hangleton graveyard. A world of magic that Team Europe must navigate without inherent magical ability.
movies.description: The Triwizard Tournament is a cover for something darker. Lord Voldemort is being resurrected in the graveyard using dark ritual. The team has been inserted into this world and must survive a magical environment without magic of their own, prevent the resurrection if possible, and escape before Death Eaters close all exits.
key_threats: Death Eaters — highly trained dark wizards. Killing Curse is instant death.
Hungarian Horntail Dragon — used in the first task. Aggressive and fire-breathing.
Grindylows and the Mer-people in the lake — Second task.
The maze in the final task — filled with dangerous magical creatures and enchantments.
Voldemort — resurrected at near-full power. Not yet at peak strength but still devastating.
movies.main_objective: Prevent Voldemort's full resurrection or, if it occurs, destroy the Portkey and escape before Death Eater forces consolidate.
optional_objectives: Capture a magical artifact for the System (+300 pts)
Assist Harry Potter in surviving the tournament (+400 pts)
Obtain a magical item that functions in the real world (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Non-magical team in a magical world. This is deliberately difficult without magical ability. God's Space provides items that create anti-magic barriers or nullification fields as potential preparations. Emphasize creative use of real-world technology against magical foes.

movies.title: Transformers
movies.setting: Various US cities and the Hoover Dam facility. The scale of battle is enormous — city blocks destroyed, Decepticons versus Autobots.
movies.description: The Decepticons are hunting the Allspark — a cosmic artifact with the power to create or destroy any machinery. The team is caught in the crossfire of a war between two factions of transforming alien robots. The team must survive in an environment where ordinary cars can be predators and skyscrapers can fall.
key_threats: Decepticons — immense mechanical beings with devastating weapons. Direct combat is suicide.
Megatron — the Decepticon leader. Immensely powerful and aggressive.
Urban destruction — falling buildings, explosions, fire. Environmental hazards are constant.
Military confusion — both sides fire indiscriminately.
movies.main_objective: Secure the Allspark and ensure it is destroyed rather than captured by Megatron.
optional_objectives: Establish communication with the Autobots (+200 pts)
Protect civilian lives during the battle (+100 pts each, max 5)
Sabotage a Decepticon vehicle (+300 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Survive in the chaos of a giant robot war. Players can't fight the robots — they need to move quickly, find cover, and complete objectives in the chaos. Autobots can be allies if properly contacted.

movies.title: 28 Days Later
movies.setting: Post-outbreak Britain. Abandoned London, desolate highways, a militarized country estate in Manchester. The Rage virus has swept the country in 28 days.
movies.description: Britain has been devastated by the Rage virus — a pathogen transmitted by blood contact that creates hyper-aggressive killing machines within 20 seconds of infection. The infected sprint. They never sleep. They never stop. The team must cross Britain to reach a military checkpoint, only to find the military may be a threat as dangerous as the infected.
key_threats: Infected — not slow zombies. They sprint, attack instantly, and never hesitate. Rage is transmitted by a single drop of blood.
The 20-second infection window — there is almost no time between exposure and turning.
The military unit at the checkpoint — soldiers who have chosen brutality as survival.
Food and water scarcity — starvation is a real threat in the abandoned country.
Rain and cold — exposure in the British countryside.
movies.main_objective: Reach the military checkpoint, assess its safety, and secure a path out of Britain.
optional_objectives: Find and evacuate civilians (+150 pts each, max 4)
Map a safe route through London (+300 pts)
Obtain a vehicle and fuel reserve (+200 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Perfect for European teams — feels closest to home. The infected are fast and deadly but not supernatural. The real horror is the human element — the military checkpoint reveals what people become without civilization. Emphasize isolation and scarcity.

movies.title: The Thing
movies.setting: Outpost 31, Antarctica. A remote research base in the harshest environment on Earth. Isolation, -60°C outside, and nowhere to run.
movies.description: An alien life form has been thawed from the Antarctic ice and is loose in an American research station. The Thing can perfectly imitate any living creature. It absorbs organisms and recreates them down to the cellular level. Any team member could be an alien duplicate. Trust is impossible. And the outside temperature is fatal.
key_threats: The Thing — an alien shapeshifter. It could be anyone or anything. Detection only possible via blood test or catching it mid-transformation.
The Antarctic environment — exposure kills within minutes. The base is the only shelter.
Paranoia — friendly fire between team members is a constant risk.
Transformation attacks — The Thing is horrifically powerful when it reveals itself.
Limited supplies and fuel for heat.
movies.main_objective: Identify and destroy all Things, survive until rescue can be arranged, prevent the Thing from reaching civilization.
optional_objectives: Identify all infected members through blood test before confrontation (+500 pts)
Recover alien biological data for the System (+400 pts)
Keep all genuinely human team members alive (+300 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: The paranoia mechanic is the centerpiece. The GM should secretly determine which NPCs are Things and let the mystery unfold. The blood test scene is iconic and should be dramatic. Outside is death. Inside is suspected death. Perfect horror isolation.

movies.title: Event Horizon
movies.setting: 2047. The deep space research vessel Event Horizon, returned from where it went — somewhere beyond the known universe. Deep space, far from any rescue.
movies.description: The experimental starship Event Horizon disappeared 7 years ago after testing a gravity drive that folded space. It has returned — damaged, with the original crew dead in horrific ways. The rescue team boards to find answers, and discovers the ship went through a dimension of pure evil. The ship itself is now alive, malevolent, and feeding on the fears and guilt of those aboard.
key_threats: The ship itself — a psychic entity feeding on trauma. It shows people their worst memories and fears made flesh.
Reality distortion — the ship shifts corridors, creates false situations.
Hallucinations vs. reality — players cannot trust their perceptions.
The original crew — dead but not gone. The ship can animate corpses.
Deep space rescue timeline — any rescue ship is 72 hours away.
movies.main_objective: Shut down the gravity drive to prevent the ship from going back to the hell dimension, survive long enough for rescue or escape.
optional_objectives: Recover the Event Horizon's black box log (+300 pts)
Save crew members from the ship's influence (+200 pts each)
Destroy the gravity drive completely (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Pure psychological horror in space. Use characters' backstories and fears. The ship shows each person their worst moment. There should be a point where players question what is real. The horror escalates — it starts subtle and becomes overwhelming.

movies.title: I Am Legend
movies.setting: New York City, 2012. Three years after a modified measles virus wiped out most of humanity. The city is overgrown, abandoned, and ruled by Darkseekers — infected humans who devolve at night.
movies.description: A modified cancer cure went wrong and killed 90% of humanity. The remaining 10% were eaten by the Darkseeker survivors — photosensitive mutant predators of extraordinary strength. The team wakes in an abandoned Manhattan. They must locate supplies, find a working vehicle, and reach the survivor colony rumored to be in Vermont — all while avoiding Darkseeker packs that emerge at sundown.
key_threats: Darkseekers — former humans. Extremely fast and strong. Photosensitive — UV light burns them. Total killing frenzy at night.
The Darkseeker alpha — larger, smarter, and capable of basic traps.
Nightfall — absolute darkness in the city means Darkseeker territory.
Structural instability — three years of abandonment has made many buildings deadly.
Food and UV lamp fuel scarcity.
movies.main_objective: Survive three days, find a working vehicle with fuel, and escape Manhattan.
optional_objectives: Recover viable antigen samples from Dr. Neville's lab (+400 pts)
Destroy the Darkseeker nest in Grand Central (+500 pts — very dangerous)
Find other human survivors (+200 pts each, max 3)
movies.notes_for_gm: Classic survival horror. Day = relatively safe exploration. Night = deadly. Time management is critical. The Darkseekers become more aggressive each night as they learn the team's patterns. UV lights, flares, and fire are the primary defenses.

movies.title: A Quiet Place
movies.setting: Rural New York State, 2020. A post-apocalyptic world 89 days after alien creatures devastated civilization. A farmhouse, a waterfall, sand-covered paths, and deadly silence.
movies.description: Creatures from another world hunt entirely by sound. They are blind, but possess hearing of extraordinary sensitivity and speed of extraordinary lethality. They are nearly invulnerable to conventional weapons. The team must survive in absolute silence — every footstep, breath, and whisper could mean death. And the team cannot warn each other with words.
key_threats: The creatures — alien hunters, blind, hunt purely by sound. Lightning-fast and virtually armored. Standard firearms only attract more.
A single loud noise will bring them within seconds.
Communication is severely restricted — sign language or absolute silence only.
Survival needs — eating without noise, moving without noise, doing everything silently.
The creatures' weakness — specific high-frequency sounds disrupt them temporarily.
movies.main_objective: Discover the creatures' weakness, acquire a means to exploit it, and find a defensible position or escape route.
optional_objectives: Map the creature patrol routes (+300 pts)
Establish a silent communication system within the team (+200 pts)
Rescue survivors from a nearby farm (+300 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: The silence mechanic is the entire game. Every action, every item the player picks up, must be narrated with sound awareness. Combat is almost impossible — players need to use the environment (waterfalls mask sound). High tension, constant awareness required.

movies.title: World War Z
movies.setting: Philadelphia, South Korea military base, Jerusalem (walled city under siege), and a WHO research facility in Wales. Global scale pandemic.
movies.description: A zombie pandemic has swept the world in days. These are not slow shamblers — they run, swarm, and climb as living ladders. Billions are infected. The team must travel from one global hot spot to the next, following a lead that might identify Patient Zero — and with it, a potential cure or defense.
key_threats: Swarm zombies — they move as a mass, build zombie pyramids to scale walls. Overwhelming in open areas.
The speed — they sprint and bite before you can react.
Global collapse — no stable infrastructure, constant movement required.
The Jerusalem fall — a city of millions overwhelmed in minutes.
movies.main_objective: Reach the WHO facility in Wales and assist in identifying a camouflage against the zombies (terminal ill patients are ignored by zombies).
optional_objectives: Evacuate the Jerusalem survivor group (+300 pts)
Collect biological samples from multiple geographic zones (+400 pts)
Identify a Patient Zero location (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: European team advantage — the WHO facility is in Wales. The swarm mechanic means open areas are death. Buildings, vehicles, and elevated positions are key. Movement between locations is as dangerous as each destination.

movies.title: Get Out
movies.setting: An upscale country estate in rural New England. Beautiful on the surface, deeply wrong underneath.
movies.description: The team is invited to an exclusive gathering at a wealthy estate. The hosts and guests are charming, sophisticated — and conducting a horrific medical program that surgically transplants the brains of wealthy clients into the bodies of unwitting victims, trapping the original personality in a helpless 'sunken place.' The horror reveals itself gradually.
key_threats: The hypnosis technique — can trap a victim in the 'sunken place' (paralyzed helplessness while their body is controlled). Sound-triggered.
The surgically modified victims — physically modified people acting as servants/security.
The participating guests — seemingly normal wealthy people who are knowingly complicit.
The revelation — once the team understands what's happening, escape routes are already cut off.
Trust — the threat appears friendly until it's too late.
movies.main_objective: Identify the threat, avoid capture, and escape or destroy the operation.
optional_objectives: Free all modified victims (+200 pts each, max 4)
Recover evidence of the operation for external use (+300 pts)
Destroy the medical facility completely (+400 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Social horror. The team shouldn't realize the threat until partway through. Early signs should be subtle — guests staring too long, hypnotic triggers during dinner, staff with glazed eyes. The horror is the gradual reveal. Combat only becomes relevant in the third act.

movies.title: It (Chapter One)
movies.setting: Derry, Maine, 1989. A seemingly normal small town, the storm drain system, and an abandoned house on Neibolt Street. Every 27 years, Pennywise wakes.
movies.description: Pennywise the Dancing Clown — an ancient entity older than humanity — resurfaces in the town of Derry every 27 years to feed on children, sustained by their fear. The team has arrived during an active feeding cycle. Pennywise can manifest as any phobia. It can only be wounded when it is feared less than it fears being hurt. Courage is literally the weapon.
key_threats: Pennywise/It — a shape-shifting entity that manifests as each person's deepest fear. Nearly invulnerable to conventional weapons.
The town itself — adults in Derry are unconsciously suppressed from helping or noticing the danger.
The sewer lair — a labyrinth of tunnels under Derry with a deadlights chamber that traps victims.
Psychological assault — It makes people see their worst fears.
Cultists — some Derry adults are knowingly complicit.
movies.main_objective: Defeat Pennywise in its lair, force it into its natural form, and destroy it during its vulnerable cycle.
optional_objectives: Rescue all missing children from the deadlights chamber (+300 pts each)
Expose the adult complicity in Derry (+300 pts)
Map the complete sewer system (+200 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Fear is the mechanic. Pennywise becomes weaker when the team shows genuine courage and unity. Have each player's fear manifest at a critical moment. The deadlights are an instant-incapacitation trap — very dangerous. Pennywise should be terrifying in early encounters and progressively less overwhelming as the team unifies.

movies.title: The Descent
movies.setting: The Appalachian Mountains, North Carolina. An unexplored cave system — vertical descents, narrow passages, underground rivers, and absolute darkness.
movies.description: The team descends into an unmapped cave system only to discover they are not alone. The cave is inhabited by Crawlers — pale, eyeless humanoid predators perfectly adapted to the dark. They hear everything, move through rock passages like water, and hunt as a pack. The team is deep underground, their ropes have been cut, and the only way out may not exist.
key_threats: Crawlers — eyeless humanoids with echolocation. Blindingly fast in enclosed spaces. Hunt by sound and smell.
The cave itself — collapses, underground rivers, sheer drops. The environment is as deadly as the creatures.
Darkness — limited light sources, and light attracts Crawlers.
Psychological breakdown — claustrophobia, isolation, and the horror of discovering what's down here.
No map — the cave is unmapped, and the route out is unknown.
movies.main_objective: Find a second exit (the original entrance is inaccessible), survive the Crawlers, and escape to the surface.
optional_objectives: Map the cave section for the System (+300 pts)
Capture a live Crawler specimen (+500 pts — extreme difficulty)
Eliminate the Crawler nest protecting the exit (+400 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Extreme claustrophobia and darkness. Light management is critical — too much light attracts crawlers, too little and players can't see where they're going. The cave should feel genuinely dangerous as an environment before the creatures even appear.

movies.title: Annihilation
movies.setting: Area X — The Shimmer, a growing alien zone on the Florida coast. Inside The Shimmer, DNA from all organisms intermingles. Plants grow in human shapes. Animals carry human vocal cords.
movies.description: An alien signal is transforming the natural world inside an expanding zone called The Shimmer. Every expedition into it has failed — most return corrupted or not at all. Inside, the rules of biology no longer apply. The team enters to find the source and stop the expansion. They find that The Shimmer changes them as well, slowly.
key_threats: The environment itself — mutations of all forms. Plants that look like people. Animals with human sounds.
The Humanoid — a creature that can replicate a person's exact movements and voice.
The Bear — a mutant grizzly bear that screams with the voice of its last victim.
Cellular mutation — extended time in The Shimmer begins changing team members at the DNA level.
The Lighthouse — the origin point, where reality is most unstable and most dangerous.
movies.main_objective: Reach the Lighthouse, understand the source of The Shimmer, and find a way to stop or escape it.
optional_objectives: Document all mutation types for the System (+400 pts)
Resist cellular mutation for the full duration (+500 pts — very hard)
Destroy the Lighthouse singularity (+600 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Most psychologically complex movie. Reality bends. Characters begin transforming. The horror is gradual and deeply unsettling. The Bear encounter is a must — it should be terrifying. The Lighthouse is the hardest encounter. Players who reach it should feel like they've already lost part of themselves.

movies.title: Bird Box
movies.setting: San Francisco and the surrounding river wilderness. An entity has appeared that, when seen by a sane person, drives them to immediate violent self-destruction. The city has collapsed in hours.
movies.description: Invisible entities have emerged worldwide. Looking at them without prior madness causes instant suicidal compulsion in sane individuals. Within hours, civilization has largely collapsed. The team must navigate a hostile world blindfolded, find the safe colony rumored to exist, and avoid both the entities and the mad people who now serve them.
key_threats: The entities — invisible. Cannot be fought. Instant death on sight for sane people. Must keep eyes covered outdoors.
The afflicted — people who were already severely mentally ill and are immune, now actively trying to force others to see.
Navigation blindfolded — rivers, forests, other humans, all without sight.
Scarcity — post-collapse world with limited resources.
movies.main_objective: Navigate 80 miles blindfolded to the safe colony.
optional_objectives: Map a safe route for others to follow (+300 pts)
Find a method to detect entity presence before it becomes a threat (+400 pts)
Bring additional survivors to the colony (+200 pts each)
movies.notes_for_gm: Unique navigation challenge. Consider how every action changes when sight isn't available. Describe sounds, touch, smell. The afflicted are dangerous in a different way than the entities — they're human, cunning, and deeply wrong.

movies.title: Train to Busan
movies.setting: South Korea — primarily aboard the KTX express train from Seoul to Busan. Confined carriages, limited supplies, and nowhere to go.
movies.description: A zombie outbreak begins on a train. The infected are fast, extremely aggressive, and one bite turns a person in minutes. The team is locked aboard a high-speed train with limited escape options. They must make it through the carriages, reach the safe cars at the front, and survive until the train reaches Busan — if Busan is even still safe.
key_threats: Train zombies — fast movers in confined space. Once they see you, the horde follows.
The confined environment — nowhere to hide, no wide open spaces.
Other survivors — desperate people whose selfishness creates as much danger as the infected.
Blind spots — compartments and tight corners where infected could be hiding.
movies.main_objective: Reach the front engine car and control the train, survive to Busan.
optional_objectives: Clear all carriages between current position and engine (+300 pts)
Protect all non-infected survivors (+150 pts each, max 5)
Find and use the train's emergency intercom to warn ahead (+200 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Classic zombie survival with a unique setting. The train's motion means the environment is always changing. Blind-spot zombies — they can't see in complete darkness — make for interesting tactics. The other survivors are morally complex, not just threats.

movies.title: Hereditary
movies.setting: A secluded family home in rural Utah. The home is filled with intricate model rooms and family secrets. A cult's summoning ritual has been in motion for generations.
movies.description: The Graham family home has been marked by the demon Paimon's cult for decades. What seems like a family's grief and unraveling sanity is actually a meticulously constructed supernatural ritual to give the demon a human host. The team arrives to find the process nearly complete. The demon Paimon is already manifesting in the environment.
key_threats: Paimon — a genuine demon of tremendous power. Already partially manifested. Utterly hostile.
Cult members — ordinary people fully committed to the ritual. They will not stop.
The ritual's completion — once Paimon fully manifests in a host, it cannot be driven back without specific countermeasures.
Psychic contamination — proximity to the ritual site causes visions, paranoia, sleepwalking, and compulsion.
The home — it is a ritual space. Every room has purpose.
movies.main_objective: Stop the ritual before Paimon fully manifests. Destroy the cult's anchor objects.
optional_objectives: Recover the ritual text to understand future occult threats (+400 pts)
Save the final host before possession completes (+500 pts)
Perform the banishment ritual successfully (+600 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: One of the most intense movies. The supernatural here is real and malevolent in a way that cannot be reasoned with. Use extreme dread, sounds in the ceiling, bodies appearing in wrong positions. The cult leader's calm certainty should be deeply unsettling.

movies.title: Us
movies.setting: Santa Cruz, California. A beach house, the boardwalk, and the underground tunnels beneath — where a parallel society of failed human duplicates has existed for decades.
movies.description: The Tethered — failed human clones created by a government experiment and abandoned in underground tunnels — have risen. Each person has a Tethered double that is their near-perfect physical match but wildly different mentally. The Tethered are coming for their above-ground counterparts. The team encounters their own duplicates.
key_threats: The Tethered duplicates — each team member has one. Same physical capability, different tactical thinking.
The golden scissors — the Tethered's weapon of choice. Silent and deeply unsettling.
Underground tunnels — vast, dark, and filled with thousands more Tethered.
The revelation — the identity swap complication creates a moral crisis.
movies.main_objective: Defeat the team's Tethered doubles and find a way to collapse the underground facility to prevent further emergence.
optional_objectives: Understand and document the Tethered program (+300 pts)
Find a non-lethal way to restrain a Tethered (+400 pts)
Reach the government files on the program (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Mirror horror. Fighting your own reflection is psychologically disturbing. The Tethered match the player's physical capabilities exactly but their combat style is different. Emphasize the uncanny valley effect.

movies.title: The Purge
movies.setting: Suburban America, 2022. A mansion in a wealthy neighborhood during the annual 12-hour Purge — when all crime is legal.
movies.description: Every year for 12 hours, all crime is legal in America. The ultra-wealthy use this time to hunt. The team is trapped outside a secured mansion during the Purge. Groups of masked wealthy killers are methodically hunting through the neighborhood. The team must survive until 7AM without shelter, weapons, or allies — in a neighborhood designed to be cleared.
key_threats: Purge hunters — wealthy, armed, methodical. They work in groups and have hunting experience.
Armored vehicles — some hunters arrive in retrofitted vehicles.
Psychological terror — masked figures, horn sirens, coordinated stalking.
The neighborhood itself — no aid coming, no police, everyone else is either hiding or hunting.
movies.main_objective: Survive from 7PM to 7AM (12 hours) and escape the Purge zone.
optional_objectives: Protect civilian survivors found during the night (+150 pts each)
Disrupt a major Purge group operation (+300 pts)
Find a weapons cache (+200 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Human-vs-human survival. This is the only movie with no supernatural elements — which makes it uniquely disturbing. The Purge hunters are clever and persistent. Emphasize the 12-hour countdown. Every noise matters.

movies.title: Don't Breathe
movies.setting: An isolated house in a derelict Detroit neighborhood. A blind veteran's home, which is an elaborate fortress.
movies.description: The team has made the catastrophic mistake of breaking into the home of a blind veteran to steal what's rumored to be a large cash sum. The man is blind — but he has converted his home into a sensory maze that he navigates perfectly in the dark. He can hear a heartbeat from across the room. He is not the victim here. And the secret in his basement is something far worse than a robbery.
key_threats: The Blind Man — a Special Forces veteran who has adapted his blindness into a superpower. He fights in the dark. He hears everything.
The home itself — converted into a tactical space optimized for someone who can't see. Traps, locked doors, disorienting design.
The basement — contains a secret that raises the moral stakes enormously.
Darkness — he turns out the lights. The team is now as blind as he is.
Sound discipline — he tracks breathing, footsteps, and heartbeats.
movies.main_objective: Escape the house alive with at least partial information about the basement secret.
optional_objectives: Escape without triggering a single trap (+300 pts)
Recover the basement evidence (+400 pts)
Escape with zero harm to any team member (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Absolute silence mechanic again, but different from A Quiet Place. This is close-quarters human threat. The Blind Man is smart, adaptive, and on his home turf. Should feel like a chess match in the dark.

movies.title: Overlord
movies.setting: Occupied France, 1944. D-Day. A small French village where the Nazi SS has conducted horrific medical experiments using a black substance found under a local church.
movies.description: A small Allied paratroop unit drops behind enemy lines the night before D-Day to destroy a radio tower. They discover the SS has weaponized a serum derived from an ancient substance under the church — it reanimates the dead and creates nearly unstoppable super-soldiers. The mission shifts from radio tower to containing a biological horror before it spreads.
key_threats: Reanimated Nazi soldiers — essentially zombies but retain some tactical thinking and SS discipline. Extremely hard to kill.
Wafner — the SS officer commanding the operation. A terrifying, precise, and cruel soldier.
The serum itself — contact with the black substance causes rapid mutation and loss of self.
Standard SS troops — before the zombies, there are plenty of well-armed Nazi soldiers.
The clock — D-Day is happening. The chaos of the invasion is both cover and danger.
movies.main_objective: Destroy the underground lab, eliminate or contain the serum source, and disable the radio tower.
optional_objectives: Recover a sample of the serum for the System (+500 pts — must be contained)
Save French civilian hostages (+150 pts each, max 5)
Destroy the entire SS compound (+400 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: War horror hybrid. The WWII setting is very familiar for European teams. The reanimated soldiers are disturbing because they still wear Nazi uniforms and walk in formation. Wafner should be terrifying as a human villain before any zombies appear.

movies.title: The Mist
movies.setting: A small town in Maine. A supermarket, the surrounding mist, and glimpses of the outside world. The Arrowhead Project military facility is nearby.
movies.description: A thick, supernatural mist descends on a small Maine town, bringing with it an assortment of interdimensional creatures. A group of survivors is trapped in a supermarket. Outside is death. Inside, a religious fanatic is using fear to build a cult that might be as dangerous as the mist. The team must find what caused the mist, stop it if possible, and survive.
key_threats: The mist creatures — ranging from large pterodactyl-like flyers to tentacled horrors to enormous lumbering giants. All are lethal.
Mrs. Carmody — a religious fanatic organizing survivors against the team. Human threat.
The supermarket itself — limited supplies, growing hysteria, and the cult dynamics.
Visibility — zero in the mist. Creatures strike from the fog.
The Arrowhead Project — the source. What caused this is still active and expanding.
movies.main_objective: Survive the supermarket, find the Arrowhead Project facility, shut down the dimensional rift.
optional_objectives: Neutralize Mrs. Carmody's influence before she becomes violent (+300 pts)
Document creature species for the System (+200 pts per species, max 5)
Shut down the Arrowhead rift without explosives (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Dual threat — creatures and humans. The creatures are Stephen King-level horrifying. But Mrs. Carmody and her cult show that fear turns people into monsters too. The mist should feel thick and utterly impenetrable.

movies.setting: The Dolphin Hotel, New York City. Room 1408 — a room that has killed 56 people in the past century without any singular entity responsible. The room itself is malevolent.
movies.description: Room 1408 at the Dolphin Hotel has a perfect kill record. No single entity haunts it. The room is evil in a fundamental, abstract way — it bends reality, replays trauma, manipulates time, and kills by exhaustion, despair, and hallucination. The team is locked inside. The hotel manager says simply: 'It's an evil f***ing room.'
key_threats: The room's reality manipulation — temperature extremes, flooded corridors, visual distortions.
Time loops — the room resets. Escape seemingly achieved multiple times before the trap closes.
Manifestations of personal trauma — the room creates tailored psychological attacks.
The minibar — a sinister detail that poisons and disorients.
56 dead — the room will not simply let the team leave. It has goals.
movies.main_objective: Escape Room 1408 without dying. Discover the room's true nature and find a way to break its hold.
optional_objectives: Record all room anomalies for the System (+300 pts)
Identify the room's source of malevolence (+400 pts)
Escape in fewer than 60 in-room minutes (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: The most mentally punishing scenario. Time in the room is not reliable. What seems like escape is another layer of the trap. Push players to their limits. Then push further. The room adapts.

movies.title: The Ritual
movies.setting: The vast, isolated forests of northern Sweden. A hiking trail that should take two days becomes a nightmare when the group takes a shortcut.
movies.description: A hiking group takes a shortcut through the dense Swedish forest and finds themselves in the territory of Moder — an ancient Norse being, neither giant nor man, that has been worshiped in these forests for centuries. Its cult of ancient Swedes lives in the trees. Its gaze paralyzes with visions. And it is always watching from just beyond sight.
key_threats: Moder — an ancient entity of enormous size that moves through the forest silently. Its gaze triggers crippling visions. Neither gods nor modern weapons affect it conventionally.
The cult — isolated Swedes who have lived in the forest for centuries, worshiping Moder. They capture people for ritual sacrifice.
The forest — dense, directionless, and actively hostile. Navigation is nearly impossible.
Psychological trauma — the entity manifests each person's worst moment and guilt.
No map, no signal, no one coming.
movies.main_objective: Escape the forest without being captured by the cult or consumed by Moder.
optional_objectives: Discover the nature of Moder for the System (+400 pts)
Destroy the cult's main sacrificial site (+300 pts)
Escape without any team member being taken by the cult (+500 pts)
movies.notes_for_gm: Folk horror at its best. The forest should feel ancient and hostile from the start. The entity should be glimpsed — never fully shown — until the final act. The cult is human but completely alien in their devotion. Isolation and guilt are the weapons here.
